preamble: The nasalized low vowel [ã] raises toward the near-open front nasalized vowel [ɛ̃] through a combination of aerodynamic factors and phonological contrast. This shift occurs historically in Romance languages as nasal vowel systems developed from Latin, and continues in modern dialects through chain shifts. Nasalization co-articulation tends to raise vowel height as the velum lowering creates aerodynamic pressure that raises the tongue body.

examples.context: Historical development in Old French period; part of modern chain shifts
Prenasal raising in Portuguese; tongue body raises during nasalized production
Gradual nasalization and raising from ~900 CE onwards; vowels raised in nasal contexts

period: Medieval-Modern (Old French period ~900+); Ongoing (modern dialect variation)
